training_directory: Directory where experiment outputs and training artefacts are stored.
enable_web_interface: Enable or disable the web UI.
server_user: Unix user used by the runtime when launching Mininet-related processes.
log_level: Logging verbosity used by the application.
random_seed: Seed used to make runs reproducible.
env_params.gym_type: Select which scenario pipeline to execute. Use *_from_dataset when replaying pre-recorded statuses JSON files; use non-dataset variants for live/synthetic traffic generation.
env_params.show_normal_traffic: Show normal traffic generation in the logs and UI.
env_params.must_check_env: Enable extra environment checks before running.
env_params.n_bins: Number of bins used by state discretization. Higher values preserve more detail but increase state-space size for tabular agents.
env_params.steps_min_percentage: Minimum fraction of episode steps required before early-stop checks are considered valid. Typical range: 0.7-0.95.
env_params.accuracy_min: Accuracy threshold used by early-stop logic. If reached (with steps_min_percentage satisfied), training can terminate before exhausting episodes.
env_params.max_steps: Maximum number of steps per episode.
ui.scenario_source_help:
Scenario source behavior:

Generate new (default): a new scenario is created when training starts.
Generate test scenario: creates a preview in memory and opens the analysis popup; no file is saved.
Load existing: click a row to select an existing scenario.json and open its popup details.

When entering Training with Load existing:
- episodes <- scenario training episodes
- max_steps <- scenario train max steps
- test_episodes <- scenario eval episodes
This sync happens only when navigating to Training, not during inspection.
env_params.attacks: Attack scheduling and mitigation parameters for ATTACKS/ATTACKS_HO/MARL attack-oriented scenarios.
env_params.attacks.likely: Base probability that a host starts an attack when idle. This value can dynamically evolve during generation depending on scenario logic.
env_params.attacks.likely_train: Attack likelihood during TRAINING episodes (ATTACKS_HO only). Range: 0.0-1.0, default 0.9. Higher values create frequent attacks for agent learning and policy development.
env_params.attacks.likely_eval: Attack likelihood during EVALUATION/TEST episodes (ATTACKS_HO only). Range: 0.0-1.0, default 0.3. Lower values provide realistic sparse attack conditions for final testing.
env_params.attacks.max_attack_percentage: Upper cap for effective attack probability (0.0-1.0). Example: 0.9 means attack likelihood can scale up to 90% in high-pressure phases.
env_params.attacks.short_attack_duration: Duration (steps) of SHORT_ATTACK windows. Lower values create bursty spikes; higher values create sustained micro-attacks.
env_params.attacks.long_attack_duration: Duration (steps) of LONG_ATTACK windows. Increase for persistent attacks; reduce for more fragmented behavior.
env_params.attacks.no_attack_timeout: Cooldown (steps) after an attack before the same host can attack again. Lower values increase attack density.
env_params.attacks.unblock_min_hold_roun: Minimum full rounds to keep a host blocked before allowing unblock.
env_params.attacks.unblock_required_norm: Consecutive NORMAL decisions required while blocked before unblocking.
env_params.attacks.apply_drop_rules: If true, the environment applies drop rules to the traffic when the agent takes the 'drop' action. This can be used to simulate the effect of blocking traffic in a real network. If false, the environment will not apply drop rules and will only provide feedback to the agent based on its actions.
env_params.attacks.include_percentage_va: ATTACKS_HO only. Controls the per-host observation size. false (default) → shape (4,): RX packets, RX bytes, TX packets, TX bytes — counters only, less noise, faster convergence. true → shape (8,): adds 4 percentage-variation columns (Δ%). Changing this value makes saved models incompatible.
env_params.attacks.thresholds: Thresholds used by rule-based labeling/detection features in attack environments.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.packets: Absolute packet-count threshold. Values above this are treated as anomalous/suspicious depending on scenario logic.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.var_packet: Allowed packet variation (%) versus baseline. Above this delta, traffic may be flagged as unstable/anomalous.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.bytes: Absolute byte-volume threshold used with packet thresholds to characterize attack intensity.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.var_bytes: Allowed byte variation (%) versus baseline. Useful for volumetric anomaly detection.
env_params.classification.thresholds: Thresholds used by classification scenarios to map traffic statistics into labels.
env_params.net_params: Network topology and SDN controller configuration used to build the Mininet environment.
env_params.net_params.start_cli: Open the Mininet CLI after creating the network.
env_params.net_params.traffic_types: Allowed normal-traffic generators. Common set: none, ping, udp, tcp.
env_params.net_params.num_hosts: Number of hosts in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.num_switches: Number of switches in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.num_iot: Number of IoT nodes in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.controller: OpenDayLight controller connection details. Ensure IP/port and credentials match your controller instance.
agents: List of agent configurations. Multiple enabled agents are trained/evaluated in the same run according to scenario mode.
agents.*.name: Display name of the agent instance.
agents.*.progress_bar: Show a progress bar while training this agent.
agents.*.skip_learn: Skip learning and use the agent only for loading or evaluation.
agents.*.show_action: Show the chosen action in the logs.
agents.*.load: If true, attempt to load model weights/artifacts before training/evaluation.
agents.*.load_dir: Relative path (from training root) to a saved run/model folder. Must contain artifacts compatible with the selected algorithm.
agents.*.save: Save the trained model at the end of the run.
agents.*.state_input_mode: Input representation for deep agents: normalized (recommended) or raw. Keep consistent with how the model was trained when loading checkpoints.
agents.*.net_arch: Neural network architecture for deep agents.
agents.*.discount_factor: Discount factor used by tabular agents.
agents.*.gamma: Discount factor used by deep agents.
agents.*.exploration_rate: Initial exploration rate for tabular agents.
agents.*.exploration_decay: Exploration decay applied after each episode.
agents.*.exploration_fraction: Fraction of training used for exploration in DQN.
agents.*.exploration_initial_eps: Initial epsilon value for DQN exploration.
agents.*.exploration_final_eps: Final epsilon value for DQN exploration.
agents.*.buffer_size: Replay buffer size used by DQN.
agents.*.batch_size: Mini-batch size used for gradient updates. Gold rule: must be batch_size less than n_steps * n_envs.
agents.*.target_update_interval: How often to update the DQN target network.
agents.*.learning_starts: Number of steps collected before learning starts.
agents.*.n_steps: Number of rollout steps used by PPO/A2C.
